_note: SAMPLE / DEMO DATA — for prototype only. Life goals a veteran can choose; each maps to sample benefit categories and suggested steps. Not official VA guidance.

goals.label: Transition out of service
goals.blurb: Leave the military with benefits enrolled, a job pipeline, and nothing falling through the cracks.
steps: Complete the DoD Transition Assistance Program (TAP) if you haven't.
Request and safeguard your DD-214 and full medical records.
File a VA disability claim (consider the Benefits Delivery at Discharge program) before separation.
Enroll in VA health care within your eligibility window.
Build a civilian resume that translates your MOS/rating into plain language.
Line up 3–5 employment leads or an education plan before your last day.

goals.label: Get a better job
goals.blurb: Find employment that pays well, values your service, and fits your life.
steps: Translate your military experience into a civilian resume and LinkedIn profile.
Register with your state American Job Center / DOL VETS employment rep.
Target veteran-friendly employers and apprenticeship programs.
Ask about VR&E (VA Readiness & Employment) if a service-connected condition affects your work.
Prepare for interviews; practice translating acronyms into results.

goals.label: Use my education benefits
goals.blurb: Turn your GI Bill or VR&E into a degree, credential, or trade.
steps: Confirm which education benefit you qualify for (Post-9/11 GI Bill, Montgomery, VR&E).
Request a Certificate of Eligibility through VA.gov.
Compare schools/programs and check Yellow Ribbon participation.
Meet with the School Certifying Official at your chosen school.
Map your program to a career outcome before you enroll.

goals.label: Buy a home
goals.blurb: Use your VA home loan benefit the smart way — no down payment, no PMI.
steps: Request your VA home loan Certificate of Eligibility.
Check your credit and get pre-approved with a VA-experienced lender.
Understand the VA funding fee and any disability-based exemption.
Work with a real-estate agent familiar with VA appraisals.
Compare with state veteran home-buying programs where you live.

goals.label: Start a business
goals.blurb: Launch or grow a veteran-owned business with the right resources behind you.
steps: Take the SBA Boots to Business course (free for the military community).
Connect with your local Veterans Business Outreach Center (VBOC).
Write a simple one-page business plan and validate demand.
Explore veteran-owned business certification (SDVOSB/VOSB) for contracting.
Set up business banking, licensing, and basic bookkeeping.

goals.label: Understand the disability process
goals.blurb: Learn how VA disability compensation works and where to get accredited help — no guarantees, just clarity.
steps: Learn the basics of how VA rates service-connected conditions (education only).
Gather your service and medical records and a symptom timeline.
Work with an accredited VSO or county veteran service officer — usually free.
Understand the claim, evidence, and appeals process at a high level.
Never pay for basic claims help you can get free from an accredited VSO.

goals.label: Prepare for retirement
goals.blurb: Line up retirement pay, survivor protection, healthcare, and a plan for your time.
steps: Review your military/retirement pay and any Survivor Benefit Plan (SBP) elections.
Coordinate VA health care with Medicare/TRICARE for Life as applicable.
Check VA and state property-tax or benefit programs for older veterans.
Update beneficiaries and estate documents.
Plan purpose and community for the next chapter, not just finances.

goals.label: Move to a new state
goals.blurb: Understand how your benefits travel — and what's specific to your new home.
steps: Find your new state's Department of Veterans Affairs and county service officer.
Transfer VA health care to a facility near your new home.
Check state-specific tax, education, and license benefits for veterans.
Update your address across VA.gov, VSOs, and benefit programs.
Reconnect with a local veteran community or VSO chapter.

goals.label: Get my documents organized
goals.blurb: Build one secure folder so you can act fast whenever an opportunity or claim comes up.
steps: Locate and back up your DD-214 in a safe, secure place.
Gather medical records, award letters, and benefit statements.
Create a secure digital folder plus one physical copy.
List your key account logins (VA.gov, benefits portals) securely.
Share access with a trusted family member or caregiver.

goals.label: Find local veteran support
goals.blurb: Connect with people and services near you — from VSOs to peer groups.
steps: Find your county veteran service officer (free benefits help).
Locate nearby VSO chapters (VFW, American Legion, DAV).
Connect with a peer support or veteran community group.
Save the Veterans Crisis Line: dial 988, then press 1.
Ask about local transportation, food, and emergency assistance if needed.
